content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

Reviewed the prompt-contract, project creation, workflow-consumer, and template changes and ran targeted tests (`cargo test --quiet task_prompt_contract -- --nocapture`, `cargo test --quiet project_create_from_bead -- --nocapture`). I found two robustness gaps in how the new contract is detected and propagated.

## Amendments

1. Require `{{task_prompt_contract}}` in the override templates, or inject the contract block outside the template body. In `src/contexts/workspace_governance/template_catalog.rs:406-444` the new placeholder is still optional for every affected stage/panel template, and `validate_template` / `render` (`src/contexts/workspace_governance/template_catalog.rs:736-742`, `src/contexts/workspace_governance/template_catalog.rs:824-828`) therefore accept override templates that omit it and expand it to empty. Because the workflow callers only surface the contract through that placeholder (`src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:159-171`, `src/contexts/workflow_composition/prompt_review.rs:351-359`, `src/contexts/workflow_composition/completion.rs:81-90`, `src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:1587-1595`), any workspace/project with a custom template silently loses the shared prompt-contract guidance.
   Rationale: This breaks the acceptance criterion on a supported customization path: the prompt generator can emit the canonical contract while stage consumers quietly stop seeing it.
2. Make contract detection top-level/fence-aware instead of matching any line anywhere. `src/contexts/project_run_record/task_prompt_contract.rs:86-87` flips on the contract whenever the exact marker text appears on any line, while the rest of the shape validator is fence-aware (`src/contexts/project_run_record/task_prompt_contract.rs:145-156`). Downstream helpers and prompt creation reuse that broad check (`src/contexts/project_run_record/task_prompt_contract.rs:113-127`, `src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:385-395`). A non-canonical prompt that merely quotes the marker inside a code block or AGENTS snippet will therefore be treated as canonical, causing stage prompts to inject contract instructions and prompt review to enforce canonical-section drift on a prompt that never actually declared the contract.
   Rationale: This is a false-positive path that changes behavior outside the intended prompt-contract scope and can make generic prompts fail contract enforcement unexpectedly.
